slice: A real, user-reported gap found via a screenshot showing a Working-View-only checkerboard triangle where an Image Experience's artwork didn't fully cover its own transform rect -- "adding new scene attached experience . i dont have any option to stretch the image to fill the scene." Investigation confirmed no non-uniform-scale Stretch mode existed anywhere in this codebase -- IMAGE_FIT_CHOICES only ever offered fit/fill/original. Fixed by adding a real Stretch option: IMAGE_FIT_CHOICES gained {value:'stretch',label:'Stretch'}; engineRuntime.js's _drawImageWithFit gained a fit==='stretch' branch drawing the image at exactly rect.w x rect.h (both axes scaled independently, no crop, no gap, at the cost of aspect-ratio distortion); renderer/slideRenderer.js's _layerDrawDecorationImage got the identical branch in lockstep so a compiled/Published Theme's Stretch choice renders correctly in Studio too. No change was needed in _syncUniversalContent or convergeSceneLayer -- both already carry a Layer's fit field through as a plain, unrestricted string. Verified via a new Playwright suite: a real, distinctly-non-square source image drawn via the real, unmodified EngineV2Runtime.load/paintLayer into a non-square rect under stretch draws at exactly that rect's own size, while the identical rect under fill (cover) draws a provably different size -- confirming Stretch is genuinely non-uniform, not an alias for Fill; a real compiled Layer Pack entry correctly carries fit:'stretch' through with its real image bytes still embedded (confirming this composes cleanly with the just-shipped Scene Decoration image fix). Both goldenBuild.js suites pass unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's own entry for the full account, including the separate zero-code-change fix for the reported screenshot's own specific gap (the existing "Fill Scene" button already resets to true full-bleed, under which Fill's cover math guarantees zero gaps regardless of aspect ratio).
